name: Holiday Cheddar Date Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F. and grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, beat the butter and brown sugar with an electric mixer on medium until well blended.
3. Add the eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in the cheddar cheese.
4. Sift the flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and cloves into a separate bowl.
5. In another medium bowl, combine the dates, pecans, cherries, and raisins. Add 2 tablespoons of the sifted dry ingredients, tossing lightly to coat the fruit and nuts.
6. Alternately beat the remaining dry mixture and milk into the butter mixture until well blended.
7. Stir in the coated fruit mixture by hand until distributed throughout the batter.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake for 2 to 2 1/2 hours in the preheated oven until the cake pulls away from the side of the pan and springs back when lightly pressed.
10. Allow cooling for 15 minutes on a wire rack, then remove from the pan. Cool completely on the wire rack.
11. Store in an airtight container for up to 6 weeks.
12. When ready to serve, cut into thin slices.
